candidates.name: Claude Code plugin manifest (marketplace.json)
candidates.description: Plugin manifest bundling MCP server + skills + metadata for 'claude plugin marketplace add' installation. Single source of truth for Claude Code plugin discovery.
candidates.home_connection: JASON-OS plugin portability. SoNash could ship its 81 skills as a Claude Code plugin.

candidates.name: Skill frontmatter: allowed-tools + disable-model-invocation
candidates.description: Frontmatter declarations that restrict tool access ('allowed-tools: Bash(qmd:*), mcp__qmd__*') and prevent auto-invocation ('disable-model-invocation: true'). Enforces behavior via manifest, not prose.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash's 81 skills use CLAUDE.md prose guardrails. Frontmatter-level enforcement is stronger.

candidates.name: MCP server as CLI subcommand (3 transports)
candidates.description: MCP server accessed via 'qmd mcp' subcommand. Three transports: stdio (default), HTTP foreground, HTTP daemon with PID file. Long-lived daemon avoids expensive model reload.
candidates.home_connection: JASON-OS Domain 02a MCP infrastructure. Pattern for shipping MCP as part of a CLI tool.

candidates.name: Query DSL with typed sub-queries (EBNF grammar)
candidates.description: Formal EBNF grammar for search queries. Typed sub-queries (lex/vec/hyde), optional intent disambiguation, parsing constraints. 182-line spec.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash's recall skill uses ad-hoc flag filters. A DSL would be more expressive across 277 candidates.

candidates.name: Scored markdown chunking algorithm
candidates.description: Break point scoring: H1=100, H2=90, H3=80, code fence=80, HR=60, paragraph=20, list=5, line=1. Squared distance decay: score × (1 - (distance/window)² × 0.7). 900 tokens/chunk with 15% overlap.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash content analysis pipeline uses naive chunking. This algorithm respects document structure.

candidates.name: RRF + position-aware blending
candidates.description: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) with original query 2x weight, top-rank bonus (+0.05 #1, +0.02 #2-3), then position-aware blend after rerank: 75/60/40% RRF weight for ranks 1-3/4-10/11+.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash synthesize skill needs fusion algorithm for cross-source candidate ranking.

candidates.name: Fixture-based eval harness with difficulty tiers
candidates.description: Test queries categorized easy/medium/hard with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends. 'qmd bench' CLI command for reproducible quality measurement.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash has no formal skill evaluation. Skills regress silently.

candidates.name: Release skill with disabled auto-invocation
candidates.description: Release workflow: gather context via script, commit outstanding, write changelog, cut release, watch CI, push on confirmation. Enforces exact dep pinning. disable-model-invocation: true.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash has ad-hoc releases. This disciplined workflow is directly portable.

candidates.name: node-llama-cpp for privacy-first local inference
candidates.description: Node.js wrapper for llama.cpp. Runs GGUF models locally with Metal/CUDA/Vulkan acceleration. createRankingContext() + rankAndSort() for cross-encoder reranking.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash's privacy-first vision could use node-llama-cpp for local AI without API keys.

candidates.name: AST-aware code chunking via tree-sitter
candidates.description: tree-sitter parsing for TS/JS/Python/Go/Rust. Chunks at function/class/import boundaries. Scores merged with regex markdown scores. Optional via '--chunk-strategy auto'.
candidates.home_connection: SoNash repo-analysis could produce better extraction candidates for code-heavy repos.

candidates.name: Daemon mode with PID file management
candidates.description: HTTP server with --daemon flag. PID at ~/.cache/qmd/mcp.pid. 'qmd mcp stop' reads PID and terminates. 'qmd status' shows 'MCP: running (PID ...)'. Idle context disposal after 5 min.
candidates.home_connection: General daemon pattern for resource-heavy services.

candidates.name: LoRA SFT on small LLMs for domain tasks
candidates.description: LoRA rank 16, alpha 32, all projection layers. ~2290 examples, 5 epochs, 2e-4 LR cosine. Qwen3-1.7B base. 92% eval score. $1.50 per run on HF Jobs A10G.
candidates.home_connection: Future reference if SoNash needs domain-specific small model. Not current need.

candidates.name: HuggingFace Jobs for cheap cloud training
candidates.description: hf jobs uv run --flavor a10g-large executes SFT in cloud for ~$1.50. Self-contained scripts in jobs/. hf jobs ps / inspect / logs / cancel for management.
candidates.home_connection: Cheap cloud training reference. Not current SoNash need.

candidates.name: Rule-based reward function for RL
candidates.description: 5-dimension scoring (Format 30, Diversity 30, HyDE 20, Quality 20, Entity -45 to +20). Hard failures = 0.0. No LLM judge — deterministic and fast, suitable as RL signal.
candidates.home_connection: Pattern for building deterministic quality metrics without LLM-as-judge.

candidates.name: Nix flake with home-manager module
candidates.description: flake.nix with homeModules.default providing programs.qmd.enable option. NixOS/home-manager users get reproducible install via config file.
candidates.home_connection: Low priority unless SoNash targets NixOS users.

anti_patterns.name: Fine-tuning before exhausting prompt engineering
anti_patterns.description: Fine-tuning is expensive and inflexible. qmd tried GEPA prompt optimization first (see finetune/experiments/gepa/best_prompt.txt) before SFT.
anti_patterns.lesson: Always exhaust prompting approaches before fine-tuning. SoNash has no current fine-tuning need but should remember this order.

anti_patterns.name: Single-runtime CI when cross-runtime is claimed
anti_patterns.description: qmd avoids this by testing both Node and Bun. If you claim cross-runtime support without matrix testing, you're shipping bugs.
anti_patterns.lesson: Match CI runtime coverage to claimed compatibility. SoNash is Node-only — don't claim Bun support without testing it.

anti_patterns.name: Caret ranges in reproducible-install packages
anti_patterns.description: qmd pins all deps to exact versions. Especially critical for tools shipping platform-specific binaries (sqlite-vec has 5 platform packages).
anti_patterns.lesson: Use exact pins for publishable packages with native deps. SoNash uses carets — worth reviewing.

anti_patterns.name: Skill without invocation gate on destructive ops
anti_patterns.description: Release/deploy/destructive skills that Claude can auto-invoke create risk. qmd's release skill uses 'disable-model-invocation: true' to prevent this.
anti_patterns.lesson: Destructive skills should declare disable-model-invocation. SoNash has skills that fire when they shouldn't (see feedback_no_premature_next_steps memory).

cross_repo_connections.detail: Both ship production MCP servers. Outline: OAuth scope-filtered, bridged to Koa API context. qmd: CLI subcommand with 3 transports. Complementary approaches — Outline for web app surface, qmd for standalone CLI.
Both have comprehensive CLAUDE.md files. ArchiveBox: 498 lines with grep-friendly naming. qmd: 167 lines with 'do not run automatically' guards. qmd is more concise.

CLI-Anything has 37 SKILL.md files for per-module AI instructions. qmd has 2 SKILL.md files with allowed-tools + disable-model-invocation frontmatter. Different scale, qmd shows frontmatter discipline.
Firecrawl extracts content from the web. qmd indexes and searches extracted content. Together: firecrawl → SoNash extraction → qmd search.
crawl4ai produces LLM-friendly markdown from web pages. qmd consumes markdown as its primary input format. Direct pipeline opportunity.
